note: The official 2025-2026 Handbook defines two concentration paths within the Programme. Both paths require 73 units of Major Courses and 12 units of Honours Project. Health Technology and Informatics comprises 9 units of Transdisciplinary Common Core, 15 units of Programme-specific Experiential Learning, 43 units of Programme-specific Knowledge/Skills Core and 6 units selected from its closed Major Elective pool. Health and Social Wellness comprises 9, 12, 46 and 6 units respectively. The official Either/Or pairs are retained in the group labels. Courses that have different roles across the two concentrations are stored once with both official roles in the group label so Programme-and-course identity remains unique. HSWB3005 is a 3-unit Summer Internship requiring 220-230 hours with a relevant local or overseas organisation or event; COMP3066 is the Health Technology and Informatics practicum; HSWB4898 and HSWB4899 are the two 6-unit Honours Project courses. University Core, General Education and Free Electives are excluded because the Programme page does not publish them as closed coded pools. No Year or Term is assigned because the official requirements table does not specify course timing. The 2026-2027 Handbook endpoint returns HTTP 401 and is not represented as verified.
